level_signals: Owns vision, strategy, and roadmap for a core product area
Expected to lead cross-functional teams and partner with Legal, Compliance, Risk

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: BA/BS in a technical or analytical field
Strong customer empathy and design thinking
Fluency in data and how to make data-driven product decisions
Experience evaluating product decisions and prioritization trade-offs
Demonstrated ability to lead and influence small teams of engineers & designers toward a common goal
Ability to collaborate with multiple stakeholders and large cross-functional teams
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Passion for AI, shown through hands-on building, prototyping, or side projects, and staying current on research and tools.
Fluency with modern prototyping tools (e.g. Cursor, Bolt, Claude Code) and comfort with design/collaboration environments like Figma and Git.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in fast-paced, high-growth startup environments
Experience building payments products or products in regulated markets
Experience building products with a significant AI component, especially if leveraging LLMs.

scope.surface: International payments products — cross-border payments, FX, global partner ecosystem, compliance/risk infrastructure, new market expansion
primary_metrics_inferred: International payment volume and corridor coverage
Unit economics improvements via partner integrations
core_responsibilities: Own the vision, strategy, and roadmap for international payments at Ramp
Drive high-velocity execution with a world-class team of engineers and designers, balancing principled intuition with data-driven decision making
Marry customer insights with technical possibilities, crafting intuitive user experiences that abstract away complexity
Help thousands of finance teams on Ramp more successfully manage their finances by expanding the capabilities of our product
Prototype rapidly using tools like Claude to accelerate the product development lifecycle and fast-forward to production-ready products.
Stay deeply plugged into AI research, industry trends, and emergent applications, filtering signal from noise and applying it creatively to Ramp's product

green_flags: Clear and specific product scope: international payments, FX, compliance, new market expansion
Specific tooling expectations listed (Cursor, Bolt, Claude Code, Figma, Git) — signals technical PM culture
Ramp is a high-growth, well-funded fintech with verifiable customer traction ($100B+ annualized spend, 50,000+ companies)
Comprehensive benefits package disclosed including full medical, 401(k) with match, and parental leave
Nice-to-haves are clearly separated from must-haves, reducing ambiguity
Payments domain experience is a nice-to-have, not a hard requirement — opens pool of applicants
red_flags: Location type is ambiguous — JD mentions relocation to NYC or SF but does not explicitly state remote eligibility or hybrid policy
3-year minimum PM experience is relatively low for the scope described (owning full international payments strategy), suggesting possible title/scope mismatch or aggressive hiring bar expectations
